title: RateLimiter unbounded growth between prune intervals
description: The RateLimiter in ratelimit.rs prunes stale entries every 10 minutes (configured in server.rs:130), but between prunes, the HashMap grows without bound — one entry per unique IP. There is no inline size check, so a burst of requests from many IPs (e.g. a botnet scan) will grow memory until the next prune. The prune interval is reasonable and the entries are small (String + f64 + Instant), so this is not critical, but worth noting that the data structure has no cap. A one-line capacity check in try_acquire before inserting (e.g. refuse new entries when len > 100_000) would bound worst-case memory.
close_reason: RateLimiter prunes every 10 minutes. At 60 req/min/IP, worst case is 600 entries per IP between prunes. With the prune cycle, this is bounded and acceptable.
